decision_stages.decision_made: LLM_DIRECT vs LOCAL_DB_ONLY vs SQL_PLUS_API vs API_ONLY.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did this correctly decide whether the prompt needs SQL/API evidence?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: can keep conceptual prompts out of the data pipeline or send data questions to tools
extended_questions: Did router send data questions to evidence tools?
Did it correctly avoid SQL/API for conceptual prompts?
Did keyword routing miss synonyms?
known_failure_examples: synonyms such as data models or broken things can be under-specified
decision_stages.name: Prompt router
decision_stages.possible_improvement: calibrate with deterministic route confidence and relevance margins
decision_stages.safety_risk: direct answers for evidence-needed prompts would be unsupported

decision_stages.decision_made: direct/simple handling vs USE_DATA_PIPELINE.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did it avoid direct answers for evidence-needed data questions?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: prevents unsupported answers for data questions
extended_questions: Does simple handling ever trigger for factual data questions?
known_failure_examples: ambiguous conceptual/data prompts
decision_stages.name: Simple prompt gate
decision_stages.possible_improvement: tighten conceptual prompt definitions without weakening evidence routing
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: direct response when suggested_action is USE_DATA_PIPELINE
decision_stages.safety_risk: unsupported claims if data prompts bypass tools

decision_stages.decision_made: rewrite/no rewrite, singular/plural normalization, synonym normalization.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did normalization preserve meaning while improving matchability?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: feeds router, token extraction, and template matching
extended_questions: Did singular/plural normalization help route matching without changing meaning?
known_failure_examples: over-normalization can erase user phrasing
decision_stages.name: Query normalization
decision_stages.possible_improvement: record before/after tokens and preserve quoted entities
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: raw query is retained for answer wording
decision_stages.safety_risk: meaning drift

decision_stages.decision_made: domain/status/date/id/entity extraction.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did it capture the important user intent signals?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: drives answer families and SQL/API filters
extended_questions: Were names, IDs, dates, status words, and entity words captured?
known_failure_examples: terse prompts and typos
decision_stages.name: Query token extraction
decision_stages.possible_improvement: add typo-tolerant synonyms only when generalizable
decision_stages.safety_risk: wrong entity/status filters

decision_stages.decision_made: domain_type, route_type, confidence, candidate tables/APIs.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Is router confidence calibrated against actual correctness?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: seeds QueryAnalysis, context, and plan families
extended_questions: Are low-confidence rows actually wrong, or just short prompts?
Does confidence predict strict score?
known_failure_examples: low confidence may still be correct; high confidence can still have weak answer shape
decision_stages.name: Deterministic QueryRouter
decision_stages.possible_improvement: combine route confidence with relevance margin and template support
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: implicit keyword scores in route reason
decision_stages.safety_risk: misrouting can cause wrong tool path

decision_stages.decision_made: answer_family, SQL template, API templates, lookup path, API need decision.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did analysis choose the correct answer family and tool family?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: controls SQL/API template families and answer slots
extended_questions: Did the chosen answer family match COUNT/LIST/STATUS/DATE intent?
known_failure_examples: correct SQL with weak answer family
decision_stages.name: QueryAnalysis
decision_stages.possible_improvement: audit answer-family alignment before changing templates
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: not fully logged; plan ensemble captures some alternatives
decision_stages.safety_risk: wrong family can silently produce weak answers

decision_stages.decision_made: top tables, top APIs, top answer families, join hints.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did selected context include the true needed source?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: limits context and template candidates
extended_questions: Were top-2 candidates close?
Were wrong tables selected due to missing synonyms?
known_failure_examples: synonym gaps can push true source out of top-k
decision_stages.name: Relevance scoring
decision_stages.possible_improvement: log top-2 margin and compare selected context to executed SQL/API
decision_stages.safety_risk: wrong table/API context

decision_stages.decision_made: selected tables, selected columns, selected APIs, compact/full context.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did context include enough evidence while avoiding token noise?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: sets prompt and trajectory evidence surface
extended_questions: Did selected context include required columns?
Did compact context hurt any query?
known_failure_examples: irrelevant API context can add dry-run noise
decision_stages.name: Metadata/context selection
decision_stages.possible_improvement: require template-required columns in selected context
decision_stages.safety_risk: missing columns or too much noise

decision_stages.decision_made: SQL_FIRST_API_VERIFY plan, SQL/API order, fast path/template/generic plan.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did the plan pick the right SQL/API path?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: determines executable tool calls
extended_questions: Did losing candidates have better answer potential?
known_failure_examples: optional dry-run API can hurt answer wording
decision_stages.name: Plan generation
decision_stages.possible_improvement: compare plan family to strict component failures
decision_stages.safety_risk: invalid SQL/API if validators miss issues

decision_stages.decision_made: selected candidate, rejected candidates, candidate scores.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did the selected plan actually lead to better strict score?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: executes exactly one selected plan
extended_questions: Are candidate scores aligned with strict score?
known_failure_examples: validator-safe plans may still be answer-weak
decision_stages.name: Plan ensemble selection
decision_stages.possible_improvement: add answer-shape/evidence completeness scoring in isolated trials
decision_stages.safety_risk: executing multiple plans would violate cost assumptions

decision_stages.decision_made: API_REQUIRED, API_OPTIONAL, API_SKIP.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Are optional dry-run API calls helping or hurting answer score?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: can add API calls and dry-run caveats
extended_questions: When SQL fully answers, does dry-run API hurt answer score?
known_failure_examples: SQL fully answers but answer emphasizes unavailable API
decision_stages.name: Evidence policy
decision_stages.possible_improvement: isolated dry-run skip/wording variants when SQL answer is complete
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: API optional vs skipped not always logged as alternative
decision_stages.safety_risk: skipping truly required API would lose evidence

decision_stages.decision_made: max SQL calls, max API calls, total tool limit.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Are tool calls being spent where they improve correctness?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: affects efficiency penalty and answer evidence
extended_questions: Are extra API calls improving correctness?
known_failure_examples: dry-run calls add cost without evidence
decision_stages.name: Tool-call budget
decision_stages.possible_improvement: penalize optional dry-run when SQL evidence is complete
decision_stages.safety_risk: over-pruning useful evidence

decision_stages.decision_made: safe or blocked SQL, selected tables/columns, unknown table/column.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Are validators blocking unsafe SQL while preserving valid useful SQL?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: prevents unsafe SQL execution
extended_questions: Are validators blocking unsafe SQL while preserving valid useful SQL?
known_failure_examples: unknown columns or template drift
decision_stages.name: SQL validation / AST validation
decision_stages.possible_improvement: improve validators only by making valid/invalid separation clearer
decision_stages.safety_risk: weakening validators is prohibited

decision_stages.decision_made: endpoint allowed or blocked, dry-run behavior.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Are API calls valid and useful under missing live credentials?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: permits catalog-safe Adobe API calls or dry-run records
extended_questions: Which queries truly need live API?
known_failure_examples: unresolved path parameters and optional dry-run calls
decision_stages.name: API validation
decision_stages.possible_improvement: endpoint-family trials only if strict and safety gates pass
decision_stages.safety_risk: unsafe or fabricated API evidence

decision_stages.decision_made: SQL result and API result/dry-run result.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did execution produce useful evidence or just cost?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: feeds EvidenceBus and final answer
extended_questions: Did SQL/API results contain enough evidence to answer directly?
known_failure_examples: dry-run API unavailable under missing credentials
decision_stages.name: Execution
decision_stages.possible_improvement: answer wording improvements for dry-run limitations
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: not applicable because exactly one plan executes
decision_stages.safety_risk: fabricated live API evidence is prohibited

decision_stages.decision_made: forwarded SQL/API facts, IDs/names/counts/timestamps/statuses.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did the right evidence reach answer synthesis?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: controls grounded claims in final answer
extended_questions: Were IDs, names, counts, statuses, and dates forwarded?
known_failure_examples: SQL fact present but answer omits it
decision_stages.name: EvidenceBus
decision_stages.possible_improvement: answer-only rewrites with unchanged SQL/API/evidence hashes
decision_stages.safety_risk: unsupported claims

decision_stages.decision_made: answer intent and count/list/status/date/yes-no shape.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did slots correctly represent the user’s requested answer type?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: affects answer template and shape
extended_questions: Does the slot shape match COUNT/LIST/STATUS/DATE intent?
known_failure_examples: COUNT evidence answered with vague summary
decision_stages.name: Answer slots
decision_stages.possible_improvement: COUNT/LIST/STATUS/WHEN template trials
decision_stages.safety_risk: shape fixes must preserve evidence hashes

decision_stages.decision_made: final answer wording and dry-run caveat.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Does the answer directly answer the prompt using evidence?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: dominant current correctness bottleneck
extended_questions: Is the answer too vague?
Does it mention dry-run unnecessarily?
known_failure_examples: vague dry-run wording despite complete SQL evidence
decision_stages.name: Answer synthesis
decision_stages.possible_improvement: answer-only rewrite variants preserving SQL/API/tool/evidence/dry-run hashes
decision_stages.safety_risk: unsupported answer claims

decision_stages.decision_made: supported claims, unsupported claims, selected answer candidate.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Is verifier checking groundedness and answer shape, not just literal support?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: should prefer grounded and correctly shaped answers
extended_questions: Are weak answers passing because claims are supported but badly worded?
known_failure_examples: technically supported but weakly worded answers
decision_stages.name: Answer verification/reranking
decision_stages.possible_improvement: shape-aware verifier scoring in isolated answer-only trials
decision_stages.safety_risk: rejecting good answers or accepting weak unsupported ones

decision_stages.decision_made: fields reduced, packaged trajectory unchanged enough for reproducibility.
decision_stages.diagnostic_question: Did token reduction preserve reproducibility and correctness?
decision_stages.downstream_effect: submission size and reproducibility
extended_questions: Did packaged trajectory still preserve original query, tools, answer, tokens, and runtime?
known_failure_examples: removing required trajectory fields would fail readiness/audit
decision_stages.name: Token reduction / packaging
decision_stages.possible_improvement: report-only checks unless readiness proves safe
decision_stages.rejected_alternatives_if_available: full vs reduced trajectory in packaging policy
decision_stages.safety_risk: final submission contamination or irreproducible trajectories

methodology: Diagnose decision stage first, run isolated variants, analyze helped/hurt examples, then decide without automatic promotion.
scope_controls: Generated diagnostic prompts are coverage-only and cannot claim official strict-score improvement.
Behavior-changing candidates remain isolated behind trial paths until later explicit promotion.
Answer-only variants must preserve SQL/API/tool/evidence/dry-run hashes.
